articles.title: Is Cardano The Next Hub For AI Agents?
articles.description: EMURGO, one of the founding entities behind the Cardano (ADA) blockchain, has posed critical, thought-provoking questions about the future of work. EMURGO’s shared insight revolved around Artificial Intelligence (AI) agents’ usage in the workforce. The EMURGO’s new update has hinted at the role of Cardano in the future of AI Agents. Are AI Agents the […] The post Is Cardano The Next Hub For AI...

articles.title: Bybit Hackers Launder $335,000,000 in Ethereum (ETH) With ‘No Friction, No Risk’: Report
articles.description: The perpetrators of the largest crypto heist in history have reportedly moved a large portion of the stolen funds without significant risks. On February 21st, the crypto exchange ByBit suffered large losses when a malicious actor believed to be the North Korean government-backed Lazarus Group breached one of its Ethereum (ETH) cold wallets and siphoned […] The post Bybit Hackers Launder...

articles.title: House Dems Move to Ban Presidential Memecoins with MEME Act
articles.description: House Democrats propose the MEME Act, aiming to ban public officials from launching tokens like TRUMP and tighten crypto oversight.

articles.title: Solana TVL drops 40% as SOL price risks further losses to $110
articles.description: Solana price is 41% down in 30 days, driven by decreasing memecoin activity and a plunge in its network TVL with a SOL double-top pattern playing out.

articles.title: Bitcoin Poised to Rebound Above $105,000 in March as Trump Delays Tariffs Until April
articles.description: Bitcoin is poised to rebound sharply in March and could potentially reclaim the $105,000 level, as Donald Trump has decided that tariffs will be delayed until a report is released on 1 April. In a recent interview with CNBC, White House economic adviser Kevin Hassett announced that President Donald Trump will finalize decisions on tariff policies for all countries, including Mexico and Canada,...

articles.title: Bank of America Considers Launching Stablecoin Pending New Regulations
articles.description: Bank of America’s CEO Brian Moynihan says the company is considering launching its own stablecoin. The firm anticipates comprehensive new stablecoin regulation in the US, which would give it a great opportunity. Moynihan noted that his firm was the first major US bank to launch its own mobile app alongside other technological innovations. Stablecoin adoption might have a similar transformative...
